text.title: Error Prevention Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Error Prevention. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Prefer input constraints (typed inputs, masks, range limits) to confirmation dialogs; constraints prevent the error, dialogs only delay it.
rule.rationale: Confirmations train users to click through; constraints stop the error at the source.

rule.statement: Defaults are the safest reasonable choice; users opt into risk, not out of it.
rule.rationale: Risk-by-default exploits inattention and erodes trust.

rule.statement: Inputs accept multiple reasonable formats (dates, phone numbers, currency) and normalise on submit.
rule.rationale: Strict formats produce avoidable rejection errors.

rule.statement: Confirm dialogs are reserved for truly destructive or irreversible actions; trivial confirmations train dismissal habit.
rule.rationale: Over-used dialogs become invisible to users.

rule.statement: Validation appears next to the input that caused it, not in a top-of-form summary alone.
rule.rationale: Top-of-form-only validation forces users to hunt for the problem.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-error-prevention.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
